---
title: Augmented Matrix
description: Encoding a linear system as an m×(n+1) matrix so equation operations
  become row operations.
categories:
- Linear Algebra
---

The **augmented matrix** encodes a linear system as an $m \times (n+1)$ matrix, so equation operations become row operations:

$$
\left(\begin{array}{ccc|c} a_{11} & \cdots & a_{1n} & b_1 \\ \vdots & \ddots & \vdots & \vdots \\ a_{m1} & \cdots & a_{mn} & b_m \end{array}\right)
$$

**Example.**

$$
\begin{cases} x_1 + x_2 = 1 \\ x_1 + 3x_2 = 2 \end{cases}
\iff
\left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 & 2 \end{array}\right)
\xrightarrow{R_2 \to R_2 - R_1}
\left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right)
\xrightarrow{R_2 \to \frac12 R_2}
\left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & \frac12 \end{array}\right)
$$

giving $x_2 = \tfrac12$ and $x_1 = \tfrac12$.
